gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/261)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/261)
text: الْحَيَاةُ وَ الْمَوْتُ خَلْقَانِ مِنْ خَلْقِ اللَّهِ فَإِذَا جَاءَ الْمَوْتُ فَدَخَلَ فِي الْإِنْسَانِ لَمْ يَدْخُلْ فِي شَيْ‏ءٍ إِلَّا وَ قَدْ خَرَجَتْ مِنْهُ الْحَيَاةُ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Fazalat, from Musa Bin Bakr, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The life and the death are two creatures from the creatures of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, So when the death comes and enters into the human being, it does not enter into anything except that the live exits from it’.
en.sarwar: 34. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Fadalah from Musa ibn Bakr from Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Life and death are two creatures of the creatures of Allah. When death comes then it enters in man. In whatever things death enters, life goes out of that place.’”
